description: Update the archive command to iterate all PRDs by task_prefix and archive completed ones independently, with scoped DB cleanup and per-PRD reporting.

criteria: Rust: No warnings in `cargo check` output
Rust: No warnings in `cargo clippy` output
Rust: All tests pass with `cargo test`
No breaking changes to existing APIs unless explicitly required
globalAcceptanceCriteria.description: These criteria apply to ALL implementation tasks

mergeStrategy: Merge to main after MILESTONE-FINAL passes. Squash commits optional.

priorityPhilosophy.description: The hierarchy of what matters most when implementing tasks
hierarchy: 1. PLAN - Anticipate edge cases. Tests verify boundaries work correctly
2. FUNCTIONING CODE - Pragmatic, reliable code that works according to the plan
3. CORRECTNESS - Code compiles, type-checks, passes all tests deterministically
4. CODE QUALITY - Clean code, good patterns, no warnings
principles: Ship solid working code first, then use tests to improve it
Test the boundaries and exceptions - edge cases are where bugs hide
Handle Option/Result explicitly; avoid unwrap() in production code

priorityGuidelines.medium: 21-50: Fix in current phase if time permits

acceptanceCriteria: Test: prefix with all tasks done returns true
Test: prefix with one todo task returns false
Test: prefix with zero matching tasks returns false
Test: prefix P1 does not match prefix P10 tasks (dash separator)
Test: mixed terminal states (done + skipped + irrelevant) returns true
Known-bad discriminator: test that rejects global (non-prefix-scoped) completion check
Test file compiles (tests may fail until implementation)
userStories.description: Write tests for is_prd_completed_by_prefix() BEFORE implementation. Must verify prefix scoping, zero-task handling, and terminal state logic.
edgeCases: NULL task_prefix: function should not be called with empty string — caller skips these
Prefix P1 must not match P10 tasks (dash separator prevents cross-prefix contamination)
All tasks in terminal states (done/skipped/irrelevant) = completed
Zero matching tasks = not completed (metadata exists but tasks deleted)

failureModes.expectedBehavior: Must use prefix-% not prefix% to avoid cross-prefix matching

invariants: is_prd_completed_by_prefix returns true only when non_terminal == 0 AND total > 0
userStories.notes: TDD: Write before implementation. Use make_like_pattern from crate::db::prefix. Tests go in archive.rs #[cfg(test)] module.
userStories.title: Initial tests for per-prefix completion check

acceptanceCriteria: Test: after clearing PRD A, PRD B's tasks remain with correct statuses
Test: after clearing PRD A, learnings table is untouched
Test: orphaned runs (no remaining run_tasks) are deleted
Test: shared runs (still have run_tasks from other PRD) are preserved
Test: global_state counters reset only when last PRD cleared
Test: global_state.last_task_id NULLed if it references a deleted task
Known-bad discriminator: test that rejects DELETE FROM tasks without WHERE clause
Test file compiles
userStories.description: Write tests for clear_prd_data() BEFORE implementation. Must verify only the target PRD's data is deleted, other PRDs' data is preserved, and learnings survive.
edgeCases: Run shared between two PRDs: only delete run_tasks for archived prefix, keep run if other tasks reference it
global_state.last_task_id points to a task being deleted: must NULL it out
Last PRD archived: reset global_state counters entirely

failureModes.cause: Unscoped DELETE FROM tasks (no WHERE clause)
failureModes.expectedBehavior: Only tasks matching prefix are deleted

failureModes.cause: Transaction not used — crash after partial deletion

invariants: Learnings table row count never decreases during archive
Tasks from non-archived PRDs are never modified
prd_metadata rows for non-archived PRDs are never deleted
userStories.notes: TDD: Write before implementation. Set up two PRDs with prefixed task IDs. Verify scoped deletion.
userStories.title: Initial tests for scoped PRD data cleanup

acceptanceCriteria: Test: two PRDs, one complete — only complete PRD archived
Test: two PRDs, both complete — both archived to separate folders
Test: PRD with NULL task_prefix — skipped with reason
Test: no PRD metadata — returns empty result with message
Test: dry_run=true does not move files or delete DB data
Test: progress.txt is never moved (stays in place after archive)
Test file compiles
userStories.description: Write tests for the top-level run_archive() with multiple PRDs. Must verify per-PRD iteration, skip reasons, and file archiving.
edgeCases: PRD with NULL task_prefix skipped with informative reason
progress.txt never moved even when PRDs are archived
prd_files empty for a PRD: falls back to project-name guessing

failureModes.cause: Archive folder name collision (same branch, same day)
failureModes.expectedBehavior: fs::create_dir_all handles existing dirs; files overwritten

invariants: archived field is flattened union of all per-PRD items
prds_archived.len() + prds_skipped.len() == total PRDs in DB
userStories.notes: TDD: Write before implementation. These test the rewritten run_archive() top-level function.
userStories.title: Initial tests for multi-PRD archive flow

acceptanceCriteria: PrdRecord struct has id: i64, project: String, branch: Option<String>, task_prefix: Option<String>
query_all_prds returns Vec<PrdRecord> with all rows from prd_metadata
get_project_info removed (replaced by query_all_prds)
PrdInfo struct removed
userStories.description: Replace the singleton PrdInfo struct with PrdRecord (id, project, branch, task_prefix). Add query_all_prds() that returns all rows from prd_metadata. Remove get_project_info().
userStories.notes: Use existing OptionalExtension import. Query: SELECT id, project, branch_name, task_prefix FROM prd_metadata ORDER BY id

correctness: Must handle empty prd_metadata table gracefully (return empty Vec)
style: Follow existing archive.rs patterns: private struct, map_err(TaskMgrError::DatabaseError)

userStories.title: Add PrdRecord struct and query_all_prds helper

acceptanceCriteria: Function uses make_like_pattern(prefix) for LIKE pattern
Returns false when total tasks == 0 (zero matching tasks)
Returns true only when all matching tasks are done/skipped/irrelevant
Old is_prd_completed() function removed
ESCAPE '\\' clause present in both queries
userStories.description: Add is_prd_completed_by_prefix(conn, prefix) that checks if all tasks matching the prefix are in terminal states. Remove old is_prd_completed(). Use make_like_pattern from crate::db::prefix.
userStories.notes: Import crate::db::prefix::make_like_pattern. Two queries: total count and non-terminal count, both scoped by LIKE pattern.

correctness: Must use ESCAPE clause to prevent LIKE wildcard injection
Dash separator in pattern prevents cross-prefix contamination
performance: Two simple COUNT queries — no joins needed
style: Reuse make_like_pattern — do not reimplement LIKE escaping

userStories.title: Implement is_prd_completed_by_prefix

acceptanceCriteria: query_prd_files accepts prd_id: i64 parameter
SQL uses WHERE prd_id = ? with parameterized binding
discover_archivable_files accepts prd_id: i64 parameter
progress.txt is no longer appended to discovered files
Fallback to project-name guessing still works when prd_files is empty
userStories.description: Update query_prd_files to accept prd_id: i64 instead of hardcoding 1. Update discover_archivable_files to accept prd_id. Remove progress.txt inclusion from file discovery.
userStories.notes: Minimal change: add prd_id param to both functions, update SQL, remove progress.txt line.

correctness: Parameterized SQL binding (no string interpolation for prd_id)
progress.txt must never be included in per-PRD file discovery
style: Follow existing pattern: query_prd_files returns Vec<String>

userStories.title: Update file discovery to accept prd_id parameter

acceptanceCriteria: All deletions wrapped in conn.transaction()
Deletes run_tasks WHERE task_id LIKE prefix pattern
Deletes orphaned runs using NOT EXISTS subquery
Deletes task_relationships WHERE task_id OR related_id LIKE prefix
Deletes task_files WHERE task_id LIKE prefix
Deletes tasks WHERE id LIKE prefix
Deletes prd_files WHERE prd_id = ?
Deletes prd_metadata WHERE id = ?
Resets global_state only when no PRDs remain
NULLs out last_task_id/last_run_id if they reference deleted rows
Old clear_task_data() removed
Uses conn as &mut Connection for transaction support
userStories.description: Add clear_prd_data(conn, prd_id, prefix) that deletes only the specified PRD's data in a transaction. Remove old clear_task_data(). Handle orphan runs with NOT EXISTS, and NULL out dangling global_state references.
userStories.notes: Use rusqlite::Connection::transaction(). Use make_like_pattern for prefix scoping. The NOT EXISTS subquery: DELETE FROM runs WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM run_tasks WHERE run_tasks.run_id = runs.run_id)

correctness: Transaction wrapping is mandatory — partial deletion is never acceptable
Orphan run detection must use NOT EXISTS (not NOT IN)
Dangling global_state references must be NULLed
performance: NOT EXISTS instead of NOT IN for orphan run cleanup

userStories.title: Implement clear_prd_data with transaction

acceptanceCriteria: PrdArchiveSummary has: prd_id, project, task_prefix, archive_folder, files_archived, tasks_cleared
PrdSkipReason has: prd_id, project, reason
ArchiveResult has new fields: prds_archived: Vec<PrdArchiveSummary>, prds_skipped: Vec<PrdSkipReason>
Existing ArchiveResult fields (archived, learnings_extracted, tasks_cleared, dry_run, message) preserved
All new structs derive Debug, Serialize
userStories.description: Add new types PrdArchiveSummary and PrdSkipReason. Add prds_archived and prds_skipped fields to ArchiveResult. Keep existing fields for backward compat.
userStories.notes: Additive change — no breaking impact on callers.

correctness: Existing fields must not be removed or renamed
style: Derive Debug, Serialize on all new structs

userStories.title: Add PrdArchiveSummary, PrdSkipReason to ArchiveResult

acceptanceCriteria: Iterates all PRDs from query_all_prds()
Skips PRDs with NULL task_prefix (adds to prds_skipped)
Skips incomplete PRDs (adds to prds_skipped with reason)
Archives completed PRDs: discovers files, moves them, calls clear_prd_data
Learnings extracted from progress.txt once (only if at least one PRD archived)
progress.txt is never moved
archived field is flattened union of all per-PRD ArchivedItems
tasks_cleared is sum of all per-PRD task counts
Public signature unchanged: run_archive(dir: &Path, dry_run: bool) -> TaskMgrResult<ArchiveResult>
Connection opened as mut internally for transaction support
userStories.description: Rewrite run_archive() to iterate all PRDs, check each by prefix, archive completed ones, skip others. Keep the same public signature. Handle learnings extraction once. Build ArchiveResult with per-PRD breakdowns.
userStories.notes: This is the core rewrite. open_connection returns owned Connection, so just declare it mut. Per-PRD loop: check prefix, check completion, discover files, move files, clear data.

correctness: Must extract learnings only once and only when at least one PRD archived
performance: Exit early if prd_metadata is empty
style: Follow existing archive.rs error handling patterns

userStories.title: Rewrite run_archive for per-PRD iteration

acceptanceCriteria: Shows each archived PRD with project name, prefix, and archive folder
Shows each skipped PRD with reason (incomplete, no prefix)
Shows per-PRD file list (moved/would move)
Shows aggregate summary: N of M PRDs archived
Dry-run mode uses Would prefixes
Empty result still shows informative message
userStories.description: Update format_text() to show per-PRD archive/skip status with reasons, file lists per PRD, and summary counts.
userStories.notes: Extend existing format_text. Add per-PRD section before file list.

correctness: Must handle empty archived and skipped lists gracefully
style: Match existing format_text patterns: action verbs change based on dry_run

userStories.title: Update format_text for per-PRD reporting

acceptanceCriteria: clear_prd_data called for all completed PRDs regardless of file count
Completed PRD with zero discoverable files still has DB data cleaned up
Test: completed PRD with no files on disk still clears DB data
Existing tests still pass
userStories.description: Fix: clear_prd_data is gated by !prd_items.is_empty() at line 192. A completed PRD with zero files on disk will be reported as archived but DB data persists, causing re-processing on next run. Change condition to always clear DB data for completed PRDs.
userStories.notes: Line 192: change `if !dry_run && !prd_items.is_empty()` to `if !dry_run`. Add test for completed PRD with no discoverable files.
userStories.title: Fix: clear_prd_data skipped when completed PRD has no files

acceptanceCriteria: No code duplication (DRY principle)
Functions under 30 lines (flag complex ones)
No tight coupling between modules
Code is easy to change and extend
Any issues found have corresponding REFACTOR-1-xxx tasks added to JSON
userStories.description: Analyze implementation code for DRY violations, complexity, coupling, and maintainability issues.
userStories.notes: For each issue found: 1) Add REFACTOR-1-xxx task (priority 18-19), 2) Add to MILESTONE-1 dependsOn, 3) Commit JSON changes. If no issues, mark passes:true with 'No refactoring needed'.
userStories.reviewFindings: No refactoring needed. run_archive (170 lines) is a linear orchestrator — extracting sub-functions would add indirection without clarity. clear_prd_data (100 lines) is a transactional sequence that must stay together. No DRY violations, no tight coupling. discover_archivable_files (37 lines) and extract_learnings_from_progress (35 lines) are minor overshoots with clear structure.
userStories.title: Review implementation for refactoring opportunities
